project_type: Jupyter Notebook ML project - Bengali multi-label cyberbullying classification
build_system: Jupyter notebooks run on Kaggle T4 x2 GPUs. No local build system.
test_framework: No unit tests - validation is done via training loop metrics (Val Macro-F1, loss curves). Verification is structural (notebook parses as valid JSON, code cells are syntactically valid Python).
build_command: python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('bengali-cyberbullying-lightweight-v4.ipynb')); print('Track 1 OK')" && python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('bengali-cyberbullying-transformer.ipynb')); print('Track 2 OK')"
test_command: python3 -c "import json, ast; nb=json.load(open('bengali-cyberbullying-lightweight-v4.ipynb')); [ast.parse(''.join(c['source'])) for c in nb['cells'] if c['cell_type']=='code']; print('Track 1 syntax OK')" && python3 -c "import json, ast; nb=json.load(open('bengali-cyberbullying-transformer.ipynb')); [ast.parse(''.join(c['source'])) for c in nb['cells'] if c['cell_type']=='code']; print('Track 2 syntax OK')"
verification_instructions: 1. Verify notebooks are valid JSON. 2. Verify all code cells parse as valid Python (ast.parse). 3. Review hyperparameter changes match the anti-overfitting strategy.
setup_instructions: No setup needed - editing .ipynb files directly as JSON.
environment_constraints: Cannot run the notebooks (need Kaggle T4 x2 GPUs, FastText embeddings, transformers library). Validation is structural only. The notebooks will be run on Kaggle after changes.
contribution_requirements: Changes should be targeted at reducing overfitting while maintaining or improving validation F1. Keep the same overall architecture and pipeline structure.
key_patterns: Both notebooks use a Config class in cell 2 for all hyperparameters. Training loops are in cell 14 (Track 1) and cell 9 (Track 2). Models are defined in cell 12 (Track 1) and cell 7 (Track 2). Optimizer/scheduler in cell 13 (Track 1) and cell 8 (Track 2).
directory_structure: Flat repository with 2 notebooks, 1 CSV dataset, and 1 plan.md file.
